title: Northern Illinois University Political Science Graduate Programs 2026: Fully Funded Master’s and Doctoral Opportunities in the United States
summary: Students interested in advancing their academic and professional careers in political science now have an excellent opportunity to apply for graduate studies at Northern Illinois University (NIU). Through its Department of Political Science within the College of Liberal Arts and Sciences, NIU offers Master of Arts (M.A.) and Doctor of Philosophy (Ph.D.) programs designed to prepare students for leadership roles in academia, government, public policy, international affairs, law, research, and many other professional fields. The university provides a supportive academic environment, distinguished faculty mentorship, and competitive funding opportunities for qualified graduate students. Applicants can pursue specialized studies across several major fields of political science while benefiting from assistantships, fellowships, tuition waivers, and stipends. Application Deadline January 15 While applications may sometimes be considered after the deadline, prospective students are strongly encouraged to apply before January 15. Applicants wishing to submit materials after the deadline should contact the department directly before applying. Why Study Political Science at Northern Illinois University? Northern Illinois University s Department of Political Science offers an advanced educational experience that equips students with the analytical, research, and professional skills needed to excel in a wide range of careers. The department consists of: Approximately 12 distinguished faculty members. Around 45 graduate students. A collaborative and supportive academic community. Opportunities for research, teaching, and professional development. Graduate students work closely with faculty mentors and fellow scholars while pursuing their academic and professional goals. Areas of Specialization Students can tailor their graduate studies based on their interests by selecting one of the department s major areas of study. 1. American Government and Public Law This concentration focuses on: American political institutions. Constitutional law. Public policy. Judicial processes. Political behavior. Governance and administration. 2. Comparative Politics Students explore: Political systems around the world. Democratization and political development. Comparative institutions. Political transitions. Governance across different countries and regions. 3. International Relations This specialization examines: Global politics. International security. Foreign policy. International organizations. Diplomacy. Global governance. 4. Political Theory Students engage with: Classical political thought. Contemporary political philosophy. Democratic theory. Ethics and governance. Normative approaches to political analysis. Graduate Degree Options Master of Arts (M.A.) in Political Science The M.A. program is designed for students seeking advanced political science training and can serve as preparation for: Doctoral studies. Public service careers. Policy analysis. Government positions. Research roles. Nonprofit and international organizations. Expected Completion Time Students can typically complete the Master of Arts degree within: Two years. Four semesters of full-time study. Doctor of Philosophy (Ph.D.) in Political Science The Ph.D. program provides advanced scholarly training for students interested in academic, research, and leadership careers. Completion Timeline for Students with a Master s Degree Students entering with an existing M.A. can typically complete the Ph.D. within: Five to six semesters. Completion Timeline for Students without a Master s Degree Students entering directly into the doctoral pathway can generally complete the program within: Five years. Ten semesters. Students may complete Master s degree requirements while progressing toward the Ph.D. Graduate Funding Opportunities One of the most attractive features of the Northern Illinois University Political Science graduate program is the availability of funding support for qualified students. Funding is awarded competitively and is available to applicants who maintain strong academic standing. The department also collaborates with other university units, including specialized centers and programs, to help secure additional funding opportunities. Available funding may include: Graduate assistantships. Fellowships. Tuition waivers. Research support opportunities. Graduate Assistantship Expectations Students who receive assistantships and tuition waivers are generally expected to: Enroll full time. Work with graduate faculty members. Perform assigned academic duties. Work no more than 20 hours per week. These assistantships provide valuable professional experience while helping students finance their education. Funding for Master s Degree Students Students pursuing the Master of Arts degree can typically expect: Up to two years of graduate assistantship support. Financial assistance during fall and spring semesters. A graduate stipend. A tuition waiver covering most tuition-related expenses. This funding significantly reduces educational costs while allowing students to focus on their academic goals. Funding for Doctoral Students Doctoral students may receive: Up to five years of funding support. Graduate assistantships. Tuition-related support. Professional development opportunities. The department indicates that the minimum Ph.D. assistantship award is approximately: $14,000 per academic year Applicants may contact the department for the most current stipend information. Career Opportunities After Graduation Graduates of Northern Illinois University s Political Science programs have successfully established careers across numerous disciplines and sectors. Potential career paths include: University teaching and research. Government administration. Public policy analysis. International affairs. Diplomacy. Public law and legal professions. Political consulting. Nonprofit leadership. Public service organizations. Think tanks and research institutions. The program s broad academic training enables graduates to pursue diverse professional opportunities both within and beyond academia. Supportive Academic Community Graduate students benefit from: Close faculty mentorship. Research collaboration opportunities. Professional networking. Interdisciplinary engagement. Academic conferences and scholarly activities. A welcoming graduate student community. The department s relatively small size allows students to receive personalized attention and guidance throughout their studies. How to Apply Prospective students should submit an application for admission to the Northern Illinois University Political Science graduate program. Applicants are encouraged to: Review graduate admission requirements. Prepare application materials early. Submit applications before January 15. Explore available funding opportunities. Contact the department with questions regarding admissions or assistantships. Early application is highly recommended due to the competitive nature of funding awards. APPLY HERE Final Thoughts Northern Illinois University s Political Science graduate programs offer an excellent opportunity for students seeking advanced education, specialized training, and financial support in the field of political science. With strong faculty mentorship, multiple areas of specialization, generous funding opportunities, and pathways to diverse career outcomes, the program provides a solid foundation for future scholars, policymakers, researchers, and public service professionals. Students interested in pursuing a fully funded graduate education in the United States should strongly consider applying before the January 15 deadline to maximize their chances of admission and funding consideration. For more information about this opportunity, visit here . Discover more Global Scholarship Opportunities on OFY here . The post Northern Illinois University Political Science Graduate Programs 2026: Fully Funded Master’s and Doctoral Opportunities in the United States appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: African Union Youth SRH Ambassadors Initiative 2026: Apply to Become an AUDA-NEPAD Youth SRH Ambassador
summary: Key Details Opportunity African Union Youth SRH Ambassadors Initiative 2026 Host Organization AUDA-NEPAD (African Union Development Agency) Eligible Applicants Young Africans and members of the African diaspora Age Requirement 18–35 years Application Deadline 17 July 2026 Opportunity Type Youth Ambassador Programme (Voluntary) Focus Areas Sexual and Reproductive Health (SRH), Youth Leadership, Advocacy, Community Mobilization, Digital Engagement, Youth Empowerment Benefits Recognition, training, mentorship, networking, advocacy opportunities, visibility, and sponsored participation in selected engagements Funding No financial remuneration; selected ambassadors receive leadership development, exposure, and participation opportunities Application Method Online Application Form Applications Open for the African Union Youth SRH Ambassadors Initiative Are you a passionate young African leader committed to advancing Sexual and Reproductive Health (SRH), youth empowerment, and sustainable development? The African Union Development Agency (AUDA-NEPAD) is inviting applications for the African Union Youth SRH Ambassadors Initiative 2026 , a prestigious continental opportunity designed to empower young Africans to champion health, wellbeing, and youth leadership across the continent. The application deadline is 17 July 2026 . About the AUDA-NEPAD Youth SRH Ambassadors Initiative Africa is home to the largest youth population in history, with more than 400 million young people aged 15–35 . By 2050, nearly 40% of the world s youth will live in Africa. Harnessing this demographic advantage requires strategic investments in youth development, leadership, governance, and health. Recognizing the critical role of Sexual and Reproductive Health (SRH) in Africa s development, AUDA-NEPAD has launched the Youth SRH Ambassadors Initiative to place young people at the center of efforts to achieve the continent s development goals. The initiative is guided by the Energize Africa Framework , which views young people as active partners and co-creators of change rather than passive beneficiaries. Through advocacy, awareness creation, and community engagement, selected ambassadors will help amplify youth voices and contribute to a healthier, more prosperous Africa. Why Sexual and Reproductive Health Matters for Africa s Future Sexual and Reproductive Health is more than a health issue—it is a key driver of economic growth, social development, and youth empowerment. Strengthening SRH outcomes is essential for realizing Africa s Demographic Dividend and advancing the aspirations of Agenda 2063 , particularly Goal 18: Engaged and Empowered Youth and Children. By connecting SRH advocacy with youth leadership, AUDA-NEPAD seeks to empower a new generation of changemakers who can influence policies, educate communities, and drive meaningful transformation across the continent. Roles and Responsibilities of Youth SRH Ambassadors Selected ambassadors will: Promote youth participation in Sexual and Reproductive Health advocacy. Amplify AUDA-NEPAD flagship programmes, including Energize Africa and A2DSRH. Raise awareness of key African Union frameworks and instruments. Advocate for youth health, wellbeing, and empowerment. Bridge grassroots realities with national, regional, and continental priorities. Engage communities through campaigns, digital advocacy, and youth mobilization efforts. Ensure youth perspectives contribute to shaping Africa s future development agenda. Eligibility Criteria Applicants must: Be young Africans aged 18–35 years , including members of the African diaspora. Demonstrate a strong commitment to Sexual and Reproductive Health advocacy and youth empowerment. Have an active digital presence and experience in social media engagement, content creation, or advocacy campaigns. Show the ability to mobilize peers and communities. Represent diverse regions, genders, languages, and marginalized groups across Africa. Added Advantage Knowledge of: Agenda 2063 The African Youth Charter The Maputo Protocol The African Union 2017 Demographic Dividend Roadmap will strengthen an application. Benefits of Becoming an AUDA-NEPAD Youth SRH Ambassador Successful applicants will receive: Continental Recognition Gain official recognition as an AUDA-NEPAD Youth SRH Ambassador . Advocacy Opportunities Contribute to important discussions on youth health, wellbeing, and development at regional, continental, and global levels. Enhanced Visibility Increase your profile through participation in high-level youth and SRH platforms across Africa. Training and Mentorship Access capacity-building opportunities, leadership development programmes, mentorship, and empowerment initiatives. Networking Opportunities Connect with policymakers, development practitioners, youth leaders, and influential networks across the continent. Influence Africa s Development Agenda Contribute directly to advancing Africa s Demographic Dividend and youth empowerment priorities. Sponsored Participation Selected ambassadors may receive sponsored opportunities to participate in AUDA-NEPAD youth and SRH-related engagements and events. Important Note The Youth SRH Ambassador role is voluntary and does not provide financial remuneration. However, it offers exceptional opportunities for leadership development, professional visibility, advocacy experience, networking, and meaningful contribution to Africa s youth and health agenda. How to Apply Interested candidates should submit their applications through the official AUDA-NEPAD online application platform. Application Deadline 📅 17 July 2026 Young leaders who are passionate about Sexual and Reproductive Health, youth empowerment, leadership, and Africa s development are strongly encouraged to apply and become part of a growing movement shaping the future of the continent. Final Thoughts The African Union Youth SRH Ambassadors Initiative 2026 presents an outstanding opportunity for young Africans to lead change, influence policy discussions, advocate for youth wellbeing, and contribute to achieving Africa s development aspirations. If you are passionate about SRH advocacy, youth leadership, and creating lasting impact in your community and beyond, this programme could be the perfect platform to amplify your voice and shape Africa s future. How to Apply Interested candidates should submit their applications through the official AUDA-NEPAD online application platform. The post African Union Youth SRH Ambassadors Initiative 2026: Apply to Become an AUDA-NEPAD Youth SRH Ambassador appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: ECOSOCC Kenneth Kaunda Graduate Immersion Programme 2026 in Zambia: Fully Funded Professional Development Opportunity for Young Zambian Graduates
summary: The Economic, Social and Cultural Council (ECOSOCC) of the African Union has officially opened applications for the prestigious Kenneth Kaunda Graduate Immersion Programme 2026, a unique career development opportunity designed to equip young Zambian graduates with practical experience in international governance, policy research, diplomacy, project management, and stakeholder engagement. Named in honor of Zambia’s founding President and renowned Pan-African statesman, Kenneth Kaunda, this highly competitive programme offers selected graduates the opportunity to work directly within the ECOSOCC Secretariat in Lusaka, Zambia, for a period of nine months. Participants will gain first-hand experience of how an African Union institution operates while contributing to meaningful projects that support Africa’s development agenda. The programme forms part of the ECOSOCC University Outreach Initiative and is implemented in collaboration with universities and research institutions across Zambia. It aims to nurture the next generation of African professionals while strengthening youth participation in continental governance and policy processes. About the Economic, Social and Cultural Council (ECOSOCC) The Economic, Social and Cultural Council (ECOSOCC) is the advisory organ of the African Union responsible for promoting and facilitating the active participation of African civil society organizations in the development and implementation of policies and programmes across the continent. Established in 2004 under Articles 5(i) and 22 of the African Union Constitutive Act, ECOSOCC serves as a bridge between the African Union and civil society organizations operating throughout Africa and the African Diaspora. Through its initiatives and programmes, ECOSOCC seeks to ensure that citizens, youth, academia, and civil society stakeholders contribute meaningfully to shaping Africa’s future. What is the Kenneth Kaunda Graduate Immersion Programme? The Kenneth Kaunda Graduate Immersion Programme is a professional training and work-placement initiative that provides recent graduates with direct exposure to the operations of an African Union institution. Participants are embedded within the ECOSOCC Secretariat, where they actively contribute to organizational activities and strategic initiatives while developing valuable professional competencies. Rather than serving as observers, trainees are entrusted with real responsibilities and are expected to contribute meaningfully to the Secretariat’s daily operations and long-term objectives. The programme seeks to: Build a pool of highly skilled Zambian professionals familiar with African Union systems and processes. Strengthen youth engagement in governance and development. Enhance the employability and leadership potential of young graduates. Promote knowledge sharing between academia and continental institutions. Develop future policy experts, researchers, diplomats, and development practitioners. Areas of Work and Learning Selected participants will be involved in a variety of professional assignments and activities, including: 1. Policy Research Trainees will support evidence-based policy development through: Conducting research and analysis. Reviewing policy documents. Preparing reports and briefing papers. Supporting policy formulation initiatives. 2. Project Coordination Participants will gain hands-on experience in: Programme planning. Project implementation. Monitoring and evaluation. Administrative coordination. Event management. 3. Stakeholder Engagement Trainees will work closely with various stakeholders including: Civil society organizations. Government institutions. Academic institutions. Development partners. African Union bodies. 4. Secretariat Programme Support Participants will contribute to: Organizational planning. Strategic initiatives. Communication activities. Research projects. Continental engagement programmes. Benefits of the ECOSOCC Kenneth Kaunda Graduate Immersion Programme 2026 The programme offers extensive professional and career development benefits, including: Practical Professional Experience Participants gain valuable hands-on experience in: International governance. Policy development. Programme implementation. Research and analysis. Institutional operations. Specialized Training Selected graduates will receive training in: Diplomacy. Research methodologies. Advocacy. Project management. Policy processes. Mentorship Opportunities Participants will benefit from mentorship and guidance provided by: Senior ECOSOCC personnel. African Union experts. Experienced professionals in governance and development. Exposure to High-Level Meetings Trainees may participate in: African Union meetings. Policy dialogues. International conferences. Stakeholder consultations. Regional and continental engagements. Professional Networking The programme provides access to a broad professional network across: African Union institutions. Pan-African organizations. Academic institutions. Civil society organizations. Development partners. Official Recommendation Participants who successfully complete the programme will receive an official recommendation that can significantly strengthen future academic and professional applications. Monthly Transport Stipend Although the programme does not provide a salary, successful participants receive: A monthly transport stipend. Logistical support from ECOSOCC. Transportation assistance to and from the Secretariat office. Eligibility Requirements Applicants must satisfy all of the following requirements: Be a Zambian national. Be under 35 years of age at the time of application. Hold a Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in a relevant discipline. Have obtained their degree within the last five years. Have graduated with Distinction or Merit. Demonstrate strong analytical skills. Demonstrate excellent research abilities. Possess strong writing and communication skills. Be fluent in English. Additional language proficiency in French or another African Union working language will be considered an advantage. Required Application Documents Applicants must submit the following documents: 1. Letter of Motivation The motivation letter should: Be written in English. Not exceed one page. Use font size 12. Explain interest in the programme. Outline career aspirations. Demonstrate suitability for the programme. 2. Curriculum Vitae (CV) Applicants should provide a current and detailed CV highlighting academic achievements, skills, and relevant experiences. 3. Certified Degree Certificates Applicants must submit university-certified copies of all relevant degree certificates. 4. Certified Academic Transcripts Official university-certified transcripts must be included. 5. Recommendation Letters Applicants must provide: Two signed recommendation letters. Programme Duration and Location Duration Nine months. May be extended up to one year. Duty Station ECOSOCC Secretariat Lusaka, Zambia Programme Commencement Date 1 August 2026 Supervision and Performance Assessment Participants will: Report directly to the Head of Programmes at ECOSOCC. Participate in regular performance reviews. Submit progress reports throughout the programme period. Selection Process The selection process consists of two stages: Stage One: Written Assessment Applicants will complete a written assessment evaluating their competencies and suitability. Stage Two: Interview Shortlisted candidates will be invited for interviews conducted: In person, or Via video conference. Candidates must present original academic certificates and supporting documents during the verification process. Important Deadline Application Deadline: 26 June 2026 at 23:59 Central African Time (CAT). Late or incomplete applications will not be considered. Successful candidates will be notified after the interview process. How to Apply Interested and eligible applicants should submit their applications before the deadline through the official ECOSOCC application portal. Applicants are strongly encouraged to prepare all required documents early and ensure that submissions are complete and accurate. APPLY HERE Enquiries All enquiries regarding the programme should be directed to the ECOSOCC Secretariat through: Email: recruitment@ecosocc.au.int Address: ECOSOCC Secretariat 8658 Kudu Road Kabulonga Lusaka, Zambia This graduate immersion programme represents an exceptional opportunity for ambitious young Zambian graduates seeking practical experience within the African Union system while building careers in governance, policy, diplomacy, development, and international cooperation. For more information about this opportunity, visit here . Discover more Global Internship Opportunities on OFY here . The post ECOSOCC Kenneth Kaunda Graduate Immersion Programme 2026 in Zambia: Fully Funded Professional Development Opportunity for Young Zambian Graduates appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: United Nations Population Fund Youth, Peace and Security Internship 2026 in Dakar, Senegal (Stipends are available)
summary: The United Nations Population Fund (UNFPA) has opened applications for its prestigious Youth, Peace and Security Internship Programme 2026 at the West and Central Africa Regional Office (WCARO) in Dakar, Senegal. This six-month internship offers an exceptional opportunity for students, recent graduates, and young professionals who are passionate about peacebuilding, youth engagement, humanitarian action, conflict prevention, and sustainable development. Hosted by the United Nations agency responsible for advancing sexual and reproductive health, gender equality, and youth empowerment worldwide, this internship provides hands-on experience within a multicultural and international work environment. Selected interns will work closely with the Youth, Peace and Security (YPS) Specialist and contribute to impactful regional initiatives that support young people’s participation in peacebuilding and security processes across West and Central Africa. Application Deadline: 19 June 2026 About the United Nations Population Fund UNFPA is the lead United Nations agency working to ensure that every pregnancy is wanted, every childbirth is safe, and every young person s potential is fulfilled. Through its Strategic Plan, UNFPA focuses on three transformative goals: Ending preventable maternal deaths Ending unmet need for family planning Ending gender-based violence and harmful practices The organization works closely with governments, civil society organizations, youth groups, and international partners to advance human rights, gender equality, and sustainable development. The Youth, Peace and Security Internship aligns with these priorities by strengthening youth participation in peacebuilding, mediation, conflict prevention, and humanitarian action. Internship Overview Position Details Position Title: Youth, Peace and Security Intern Organization: United Nations Population Fund (UNFPA) Regional Office: West and Central Africa Regional Office (WCARO) Location: Dakar, Senegal Internship Duration: 6 Months Employment Type: Full-Time Internship Internship Modality: In-Person (Remote arrangements may be discussed) Vacancy Number: 34675 Required Language: French Desired Language: English Additional Advantage: Portuguese and/or Spanish Application Deadline: 19 June 2026 The selected intern will work under the direct supervision of the Youth, Peace and Security Specialist while collaborating with multiple programme teams across the regional office. A structured work plan will be developed to ensure meaningful learning opportunities and measurable achievements throughout the internship period. Key Responsibilities Interns will contribute to several strategic areas of the Youth, Peace and Security agenda. 1. Research and Analysis Successful candidates will support research and evidence generation activities related to youth participation in peacebuilding and security initiatives. Responsibilities include: Conducting background research on youth participation in peacebuilding, conflict resolution, and humanitarian action Monitoring national and regional Youth, Peace and Security policies Compiling reports and policy updates Supporting the preparation of briefing notes Assisting with fact sheets and policy documents Contributing to knowledge products related to Youth, Peace and Security themes 2. Project Support Interns will assist in the implementation and coordination of regional programmes and initiatives. Key activities include: Supporting regional planning and coordination of Youth, Peace and Security projects Assisting country offices with project implementation activities Supporting youth engagement initiatives in peace processes Contributing to mediation and conflict-prevention programmes Assisting with the design of youth-led peacebuilding initiatives Supporting the delivery of training sessions and capacity-building activities 3. Communication and Advocacy The internship also provides valuable communications experience. Tasks include: Drafting social media content Preparing newsletters and blog articles Developing advocacy materials for youth-focused campaigns Supporting public awareness initiatives related to peace and security Assisting in organizing webinars and virtual dialogues Supporting workshops involving young peacebuilders and youth organizations 4. Partnership and Coordination Interns will gain exposure to stakeholder engagement and partnership management. Activities include: Supporting collaboration with youth networks Engaging civil society organizations and strategic partners Documenting best practices and lessons learned Participating in inter-agency coordination meetings Providing administrative support for Youth, Peace and Security coordination efforts Eligibility Requirements To qualify for this internship, applicants must meet UNFPA s educational requirements and demonstrate a strong interest in development and peacebuilding. Educational Requirements Applicants must satisfy at least one of the following conditions: Option A Be enrolled in a bachelor s degree programme or equivalent Have completed at least one full academic year Option B Be enrolled in a postgraduate degree programme such as a master s degree or higher Option C Have recently graduated from a university programme Begin the internship within one year of graduation if selected Option D Be enrolled in a postgraduate professional trainee programme Complete the internship as part of the programme requirements Required Skills and Qualifications Applicants should demonstrate: Strong interest in development, peace and security, and youth engagement Excellent research and analytical skills Proficiency in Microsoft Word, Excel, and PowerPoint Ability to work independently Strong teamwork and collaboration skills Good communication abilities Adaptability and cultural sensitivity Capacity to work effectively in multicultural environments Preferred Qualifications The following experiences are considered advantageous: Volunteering experience in youth-led initiatives Experience in peacebuilding programmes Humanitarian work experience Engagement in community development activities Participation in youth advocacy projects Language Requirements Applicants must demonstrate: Required Written and spoken proficiency in French Written and spoken proficiency in English Additional Assets Portuguese language skills Spanish language skills Candidates with multilingual abilities may have an added advantage in the selection process. Learning Opportunities and Professional Development One of the most attractive aspects of this internship is the extensive learning experience provided by UNFPA. Upon completion of the programme, interns will gain: Institutional Knowledge Understanding of the UNFPA mandate Knowledge of regional office structures and operations Familiarity with United Nations systems and procedures Technical Expertise Deep understanding of the Youth, Peace and Security agenda Knowledge of programme design and implementation Exposure to development and humanitarian programming Professional Skills Experience drafting substantive policy and programme documents Exposure to UN technical language and reporting standards Understanding of coordination mechanisms within the UN system Practical communication and advocacy experience Networking Opportunities Interns will have opportunities to: Engage with UN professionals Build professional networks Collaborate with experts across multiple sectors Gain international work experience Multicultural Work Experience Participants will: Work within diverse international teams Strengthen cross-cultural communication skills Develop global professional competencies Build leadership and collaboration capacities Financial Support and Stipend Information UNFPA internships are unpaid positions; however, eligible interns may receive a stipend. Important details include: A stipend may be provided to help cover basic daily expenses Stipends are available for interns who are not financially supported by another institution, scholarship, foundation, university, or government programme Stipend amounts vary depending on the agreed duty station and work arrangement Medical Insurance Requirement Applicants should note that: Medical insurance is mandatory Proof of insurance must be submitted before the internship begins UNFPA does not provide medical insurance coverage for interns Core Competencies Sought by UNFPA Successful candidates are expected to demonstrate: Values Integrity Commitment to UNFPA and the United Nations system Respect for diversity Openness to change Core Competencies Results orientation Accountability Professional expertise Strategic thinking Teamwork and relationship management Functional Competencies Analytical skills Communication skills Teamwork Client orientation Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion Commitment UNFPA is committed to creating an inclusive workplace that values diversity and equal opportunity. The organization particularly encourages applications from: Women Persons with disabilities Indigenous populations Minority ethnic groups LGBTQIA+ communities Other underrepresented populations Reasonable accommodation is available for applicants with disabilities during the recruitment process. Why This Internship Stands Out The Youth, Peace and Security Internship at the United Nations Population Fund offers a unique opportunity to contribute to regional peacebuilding initiatives while gaining practical experience within the United Nations system. Participants will work directly on issues affecting youth participation in peace and security, gain exposure to international development programming, strengthen their research and communication skills, and build valuable professional networks that can support future careers in international development, humanitarian action, peacebuilding, and public policy. For aspiring professionals seeking meaningful experience within a leading United Nations agency, this internship represents an outstanding pathway into international development and peacebuilding careers. Application Deadline 19 June 2026 CLICK HERE TO APPLY For more information about this opportunity, visit here . Discover more Global internship Opportunities on OFY here . The post United Nations Population Fund Youth, Peace and Security Internship 2026 in Dakar, Senegal (Stipends are available) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Lisle International Global Seed Grants 2026: Funding Opportunity for Intercultural Exchange and Global Citizenship Projects Worldwide
summary: Organizations working to promote intercultural understanding, global citizenship, international collaboration, and meaningful cross-cultural experiences now have an exciting opportunity to secure funding through the Lisle International Global Seed Grants 2026 . Lisle International has officially announced the opening of its Global Seed Grant application process, providing support for innovative projects that align with its mission of building world citizens through intercultural engagement and international understanding. The Global Seed Grant program is designed to help organizations launch, strengthen, and expand initiatives that foster meaningful connections across cultures, communities, and countries. Through a structured two-phase application process, selected organizations can access funding and personalized mentorship to help transform promising ideas into impactful projects. Organizations interested in applying should prepare early, as the Request to Apply (RTA) submission window may close before the official deadline if a high volume of applications is received. About Lisle International Lisle International is dedicated to supporting a global community of changemakers by promoting intercultural experiences, international collaboration, and global citizenship. Through its grant programs and international network, the organization helps individuals and institutions develop projects that bridge cultures, encourage understanding, and contribute to positive global engagement. Its flagship Global Seed Grant program provides financial support and mentoring to organizations working to create transformative intercultural initiatives around the world. What Are the Global Seed Grants? The Global Seed Grants are competitive funding opportunities designed to support projects that align with Lisle International’s mission and values. These grants aim to: Promote intercultural understanding Foster international collaboration Encourage global citizenship Support innovative educational initiatives Build connections across diverse communities Strengthen cross-cultural learning experiences Empower organizations working for positive social impact The program is particularly interested in supporting projects that demonstrate creativity, sustainability, and meaningful engagement among participants from different cultural backgrounds. How the Grant Process Works The Global Seed Grant program uses a two-phase application process . This approach allows Lisle International to identify promising projects before inviting selected applicants to submit comprehensive proposals. Phase One: Request to Apply (RTA) The first step involves submitting a Request to Apply (RTA) through Lisle International’s online grant portal. During this stage, applicants provide information about: Their organization Project concept Expected impact Alignment with Lisle International s mission Project goals and objectives The RTA serves as an initial screening process. Lisle reviews all submissions and identifies projects that demonstrate strong potential and alignment with the organization s values. Only applicants whose RTAs are approved will move forward to the second phase. Why the RTA Matters The Request to Apply is not merely an expression of interest. It serves as a critical evaluation stage where reviewers assess: Project relevance Feasibility Innovation Potential impact Organizational capacity Alignment with grant priorities Organizations should therefore invest time in preparing a strong and thoughtful RTA submission. Phase Two: Full Proposal Submission Organizations that successfully pass the RTA stage will receive an invitation to submit a full proposal. This stage allows applicants to provide more detailed information about: Project design Implementation strategy Expected outcomes Budget Monitoring and evaluation plans Sustainability measures The full proposal provides reviewers with a comprehensive understanding of the project s objectives and potential impact. Dedicated Grant Mentor Support One of the most valuable features of the Lisle International Global Seed Grant program is the mentorship component. Applicants invited to submit a full proposal are assigned a Lisle Grant Mentor . What Does a Grant Mentor Do? The mentor helps applicants: Navigate the application process Refine project concepts Strengthen proposal quality Align projects with Lisle s mission and values Clarify application requirements Address questions and concerns The mentor serves as a dedicated point of contact throughout the proposal development process. This personalized support helps organizations present stronger and more competitive grant applications. Funding Focus While Lisle International emphasizes intercultural engagement and global citizenship, applicants should carefully review the eligibility criteria to ensure that their proposed projects align with the organization s mission. Projects may involve: International education Cross-cultural exchange Global citizenship development Youth engagement Community partnerships International collaboration Cultural understanding initiatives Educational exchanges Leadership development Social impact projects with intercultural components Organizations are encouraged to explore Lisle s eligibility requirements before beginning their applications. Important Application Dates Request to Apply (RTA) Opens 📅 5 June 2026 Request to Apply (RTA) Deadline 📅 30 June 2026 However, applicants should note that: The RTA portal may close earlier than the advertised deadline if a high volume of submissions is received. For this reason, organizations are strongly encouraged to submit as early as possible. Notification of RTA Results 📅 Before the end of July 2026 Selected applicants will receive invitations to proceed to the full proposal stage. Full Proposal Deadline 📅 1 September 2026 Only organizations invited after the RTA review process may submit full proposals. Grant Recipient Announcement 📅 30 November 2026 Successful Global Seed Grant recipients will be selected during the annual Lisle Meeting and officially announced by the end of November. Project Implementation Period Funded projects are expected to be implemented during the following calendar year. Project Timeline 📅 1 January – 31 December (Project Year) Organizations should plan their activities within this implementation period. Application Deadline Timing Both the Request to Apply and the Full Proposal (for invited applicants) must be submitted no later than: 🕛 11:59 PM U.S. Eastern Time Applicants should carefully account for time zone differences when planning their submissions. Why Organizations Should Apply The Lisle International Global Seed Grant offers several significant advantages beyond funding. Financial Support Selected organizations receive seed funding to launch or strengthen impactful projects. Professional Mentorship Applicants gain access to experienced grant mentors who provide valuable guidance throughout the process. Global Network Access Organizations become connected to a broader community of changemakers and intercultural practitioners. Increased Visibility Successful projects benefit from association with a respected international organization dedicated to global citizenship. Capacity Building The structured application and mentoring process helps organizations improve project planning and grant-writing skills. Tips for a Strong Application Organizations interested in applying should: 1. Demonstrate Clear Alignment Show how the project directly supports intercultural understanding and global citizenship. 2. Focus on Impact Clearly explain the difference the project will make and who will benefit. 3. Be Specific Provide concrete objectives, activities, and outcomes rather than broad ideas. 4. Highlight Sustainability Explain how project benefits will continue after the grant period. 5. Submit Early Because the RTA portal may close before the official deadline, early submission is highly recommended. How to Apply All applications must be submitted through Lisle International s official grant portal known as Submit . Applications sent through any other method will not be considered. Organizations should carefully review the eligibility requirements and preview application materials before beginning the submission process. Contact Information For questions regarding the grant program: 📧 office@lisleinternational.org 📍 Lisle International 539 W. Commerce St #1782 Dallas, TX 75208 United States Apply Here Final Thoughts The Lisle International Global Seed Grants 2026 present an outstanding opportunity for organizations dedicated to fostering intercultural understanding, international collaboration, and global citizenship. With a supportive mentorship model, a transparent application process, and a commitment to empowering changemakers worldwide, the program offers more than funding—it provides a pathway for organizations to expand their impact and contribute to a more connected and understanding world. Organizations with innovative projects are encouraged to prepare early and submit their Request to Apply before the application window closes. For more information on this grant visit here Discover more grant opportunities on OFY here The post Lisle International Global Seed Grants 2026: Funding Opportunity for Intercultural Exchange and Global Citizenship Projects Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Lilly Foundation Open Call 2026: Grant Funding for Global Health, STEM Education, and Economic Mobility Programs
summary: The Eli Lilly and Company Foundation (Lilly Foundation) has officially launched its 2026 Open Call for Applications , providing funding opportunities for eligible charitable organizations working to improve health outcomes, strengthen education pathways, and expand economic opportunities for underserved communities. Through its annual charitable grants program, the Lilly Foundation supports nonprofit organizations whose missions align with its strategic focus areas of Global Health, K-12 STEM Education, and Economic Mobility . The Foundation seeks to fund impactful initiatives that address pressing social challenges while creating sustainable opportunities for individuals and communities to thrive. Organizations with innovative programs that improve healthcare access, strengthen STEM learning pathways, or enhance workforce and economic opportunities are encouraged to submit a pre-application during the 2026 funding cycle. About the Lilly Foundation Established in 1968, the Eli Lilly and Company Foundation was created to extend the charitable reach and social impact of Eli Lilly and Company. The Foundation operates as a tax-exempt private foundation and supports charitable initiatives that align with its philanthropic mission of improving lives and strengthening communities. Through strategic grantmaking, the Foundation partners with organizations that demonstrate measurable impact, innovation, and long-term sustainability. Lilly Foundation Open Call 2026 The Lilly Foundation s 2026 Open Call provides grant funding to eligible charitable organizations involved in initiatives that align with one or more of the Foundation s core focus areas. The Foundation is particularly interested in supporting programs that deliver meaningful outcomes for underserved and low-income communities while promoting long-term social and economic progress. Funding Focus Areas 1. Global Health The Global Health focus area supports charitable organizations working to improve access to quality healthcare services in resource-limited settings. The Foundation aims to address healthcare disparities by supporting organizations that: Improve access to healthcare services Bring healthcare closer to underserved populations Strengthen healthcare delivery systems Address barriers to treatment and care Reduce the burden of non-communicable diseases (NCDs) Improve health outcomes in vulnerable communities Priority Areas Particular priority may be given to charitable initiatives focused on: Cardiometabolic health Diabetes prevention and management Obesity prevention Cardiovascular health Healthcare system strengthening Community-based healthcare solutions Geographic Scope Programs may operate: In the United States In low-income countries In lower-middle-income countries Organizations implementing innovative healthcare solutions in underserved settings are especially encouraged to apply. 2. K-12 STEM Education The Foundation is committed to expanding educational opportunities for students from low-income communities through investments in science, technology, engineering, and mathematics (STEM) education. Program Objectives Supported initiatives may focus on: STEM learning opportunities Educational equity Academic enrichment Career readiness STEM mentorship programs Teacher development Science and technology engagement Target Population The Foundation prioritizes students from low-income communities, particularly during: Middle school years Critical educational transition periods Geographic Focus This funding area specifically supports programs serving: 📍 Marion County, Indiana, USA 3. Economic Mobility The Economic Mobility focus area seeks to increase opportunities for long-term prosperity and community well-being. The Foundation supports organizations delivering programs that strengthen economic opportunity and improve quality of life for underserved populations. Key Areas of Support Eligible programs may focus on: Workforce Readiness Skills training Career preparation Employment support services Professional development Postsecondary Education Pathways College readiness Vocational training Technical education Skills-based learning programs Housing Stability Housing support services Community development initiatives Programs promoting long-term stability Geographic Focus Funding under this category is directed toward organizations serving: 📍 Marion County, Indiana, USA Additional Community Support Initiatives Beyond the Open Call funding categories, the Lilly Foundation also contributes to community strengthening through: Employee Giving Programs Supporting charitable donations made by eligible Lilly employees. Disaster Relief Funding organizations involved in: Emergency response Humanitarian assistance Recovery efforts Community Development Supporting initiatives that address urgent local needs and strengthen community resilience. Who Can Apply? Eligible applicants generally include: Registered nonprofit organizations Charitable organizations Community-based organizations Educational institutions Foundations Public charities Organizations recognized under relevant charitable regulations Applicants should demonstrate: A clear charitable mission Alignment with Lilly Foundation priorities Strong organizational capacity Measurable program outcomes Sustainable implementation strategies What Types of Projects May Be Competitive? Organizations may be particularly competitive if they demonstrate: Strong Community Impact Programs that create measurable improvements in health, education, or economic outcomes. Scalability Solutions with the potential to expand and benefit larger populations. Innovation Creative approaches to addressing persistent social challenges. Sustainability Programs capable of generating long-term results beyond the grant period. Equity and Inclusion Initiatives designed to serve underserved and vulnerable populations. Benefits of Receiving Lilly Foundation Funding Successful organizations may benefit from: Financial support for program implementation Increased visibility and credibility Opportunities to expand impact Strengthened community partnerships Enhanced capacity for growth and innovation As one of the world s leading corporate foundations, Lilly Foundation support can help organizations scale proven solutions and improve outcomes for communities in need. Application Process The 2026 Open Call utilizes a pre-application process. Organizations interested in seeking funding should review the program guidelines and frequently asked questions before submitting their application. Applicants are encouraged to ensure their proposed activities align closely with one of the Foundation s designated funding priorities. Apply Here Important Dates Pre-Application Opens 📅 1 June 2026 Pre-Application Deadline 📅 3 July 2026 Organizations are encouraged to submit their applications well before the deadline to avoid technical issues or last-minute complications. Why Organizations Should Apply The Lilly Foundation Open Call 2026 represents an important opportunity for nonprofit organizations working at the intersection of health, education, and economic opportunity. With a strong emphasis on measurable impact, equity, and sustainable change, the Foundation seeks to partner with organizations that are creating lasting improvements in the lives of individuals and communities. Whether your organization is expanding healthcare access in underserved regions, preparing young people for STEM careers, or helping families achieve economic stability, this funding opportunity may provide valuable support to advance your mission and amplify your impact. For more information on this grant visit here Discover more grant opportunities on OFY on here The post Lilly Foundation Open Call 2026: Grant Funding for Global Health, STEM Education, and Economic Mobility Programs appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Moonshot Awards 2026 Applications Now Open for Young Changemakers Worldwide
summary: Program Name: Moonshot Awards 2026 Organizer: Moonshot Platform Website: moonshotplatform.org/awards Application Deadline: July 12, 2026, 11:59 PM Pacific Time Awards Ceremony: November 2026 in New York City, Application Link: https:// moonshotplatform.submittable. com/submit/903f88da-45c2-44b0- a71e-39bedae6bb2b/moonshot- awards-2026 The Moonshot Awards are an annual global program celebrating young innovators, founders, artists, scientists, and advocates aged 15–30 who are building bold solutions to the world s most urgent challenges. Free to apply and open to all nationalities. They serve as the entry point into the broader Moonshot ecosystem—a holistic, two-year journey that develops the person behind the work, not just the work itself. Applications are accepted across 3 main categories: Idea, Start-Up, and Nonprofit, as well as 5 thematic categories: Learning, Health, Art for Humanity, Borderless, and AI for Good. Eligibility Criteria Age 15–30 Working on an impact-driven project, idea, venture, initiative, or creative practice Contributing to at least one of the UN Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) or working toward positive social, environmental, or systemic impact Open to all nationalities, no restrictions Free to apply Individuals and teams both welcome Applicants from underrepresented communities, the Global South, refugee and displaced populations, and indigenous communities are especially encouraged to apply. Benefits The Moonshot Awards are not just recognition; they are a gateway into a full ecosystem of support built around three pillars: Connect, Embody, and Create. Equity-free grant funding to support and grow your work Moonshot Young Leaders Camp: an immersive week-long leadership retreat in Prague and Ostrava, Czech Republic, bringing together young leaders and world-class mentors Moonshot Youth Innovation Summit: a global gathering exploring bold ideas at the intersection of technology, democracy, and the future of humanity Two-year acceleration program including mentorship, skill-building, and ongoing support (valued at $30,000 per leader) Moonshot Impact Labs: online education covering organizational building, fundraising, storytelling, self-care, and more. Moonshot Global Community: a lifelong network of young changemakers, mentors, investors, and supporters from across the world Moonshot Access: opportunities to speak and be represented at leading global convenings and forums. Funded trip to the Moonshot Awards Gala in New York City Other Key Information The top 100 shortlisted candidates, not just the winners, are welcomed into the Moonshot Global Community. The top 20–25 finalists also enter the two-year acceleration program. No travel or nationality restrictions. Grants are delivered regardless of whether the recipient can attend the ceremony. Selection criteria focus on: personal connection to the problem, innovative yet accessible solutions, and meaningful potential for scalable impact Much more ecosystem-forward now. The Awards feel like a door, not a destination. APPLY HERE For more information about this opportunity, visit here . Discover more Global Award Opportunities on OFY here . The post Moonshot Awards 2026 Applications Now Open for Young Changemakers Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Johns Hopkins SAIS Public Service Fellows & Scholars Scholarships 2026–2027: Study in Washington DC with Up to 100% Tuition Funding for Future Global Leaders
summary: Are you passionate about public service, international development, diplomacy, human rights, climate action, national security, social justice, refugee issues, or global policy? Do you aspire to build a meaningful career that contributes to solving some of the world s most pressing challenges? The Johns Hopkins School of Advanced International Studies (SAIS) is offering an exceptional opportunity for future and current public service leaders through its prestigious Public Service Fellows Scholarship Program and Public Service Scholars Scholarship Program. These highly competitive scholarships support outstanding students pursuing graduate studies in international relations and public policy at one of the world s leading institutions for global affairs. The programs provide substantial tuition support, leadership development opportunities, exclusive networking events, and direct engagement with public service practitioners and policymakers. Whether you are an early-career professional seeking a fully funded master s degree or a mid-career professional looking to strengthen your policy expertise, these scholarships can help accelerate your impact and career growth. About Johns Hopkins SAIS The Johns Hopkins School of Advanced International Studies (SAIS) is part of Johns Hopkins University, America s first research university, established in 1876. Located in Washington, DC, SAIS is globally recognized for excellence in: International Relations Public Policy Diplomacy International Economics Security Studies Development Studies Global Governance Its strategic location in the United States capital provides students with direct access to policymakers, diplomats, international organizations, think tanks, and development institutions. What Are the Public Service Fellows and Scholars Programs? The Public Service Fellows and Scholars Programs were created to support talented individuals dedicated to serving society through careers in public policy, government, nonprofit leadership, international development, diplomacy, and social impact. The programs aim to: Promote leadership in public service. Support talented students from diverse backgrounds. Encourage innovative approaches to policy challenges. Foster engagement with public service practitioners. Build a community of future changemakers. Johns Hopkins SAIS specifically seeks applicants who are: Culturally aware. Adaptable. Strategic thinkers. Effective problem-solvers. Committed to public service. The programs welcome individuals interested in fields such as: Human Rights Diplomacy National Security International Development Climate Change Refugee Issues Gender Diversity Social Justice Public Policy Public Service Fellows Scholarship Program Overview The Public Service Fellows Scholarship Program is designed for highly talented graduate students pursuing the Master of Arts in International Relations (MAIR) at the Johns Hopkins SAIS Washington, DC campus. The program supports individuals who aspire to build careers dedicated to public service and global leadership. Public Service Fellows Scholarship Benefits Selected Fellows receive: 1. Full Tuition Scholarship Recipients receive: 100% tuition coverage during the first year. Renewal for the second year upon meeting academic requirements. Funding for up to four semesters. 2. Exclusive Professional Programming Participants gain access to: Leadership lectures. Policy seminars. Public service workshops. Special networking events. 3. Access to Influential Leaders Fellows engage directly with: Policy experts. Public sector leaders. Alumni. Government officials. International practitioners. 4. Career Development Support The Office of Global Careers provides: Career coaching. Public service career guidance. Professional networking opportunities. Internship and employment support. 5. Community and Volunteer Engagement Participants join social and volunteer activities designed to build: Leadership skills. Teamwork. Civic engagement. Professional relationships. Public Service Fellows Eligibility Requirements Applicants must: Be citizens of any country. Gain admission to the Master of Arts in International Relations (MAIR) program in Washington, DC. Demonstrate leadership potential. Show academic excellence. Demonstrate commitment to public service through professional experience or academic work. Special consideration is given to applicants who demonstrate financial need. Public Service Fellows Program Requirements Recipients must: Remain full-time MAIR students. Maintain a cumulative GPA of 3.4 or higher. Participate in at least one admissions recruitment activity each semester. Complete semester and final program surveys. Submit the Office of Global Careers Employment Outcomes Survey before graduation. Public Service Fellows Application Process Applicants must: Apply for admission to the MAIR program. Indicate interest in institutional financial aid. Indicate interest in a Public Service Award. Submit: Financial Aid Application. Scholarship Essay. U.S. citizens and permanent residents must complete the FAFSA using school code E00474. Public Service Scholars Scholarship Program Overview The Public Service Scholars Scholarship Program is intended for experienced mid-career professionals pursuing the Master of International Public Policy (MIPP). Applicants should have prior experience in public service and intend to continue serving communities and institutions after graduation. The scholarship is available only to Fall applicants. Public Service Scholars Benefits Selected Scholars receive: 1. Significant Tuition Support Recipients receive: Up to 50% tuition scholarship. 2. Exclusive Leadership Programming Participants gain access to: Public policy seminars. Leadership workshops. Professional networking events. Public service discussions. 3. Career Advancement Opportunities Participants receive: Guidance from the Office of Global Careers. Access to influential networks. Mentorship opportunities. Career development support. 4. Community Building Activities Scholars participate in: Volunteer initiatives. Networking events. Cohort activities. Public Service Scholars Eligibility Requirements Applicants must: Be citizens of any country. Be admitted to the MIPP program at the Washington, DC campus. Be enrolled full-time or part-time (minimum of two courses per semester). Demonstrate leadership and professional achievement. Show commitment to public service. Special consideration is also given to applicants with demonstrated financial need. Public Service Scholars Requirements Recipients must: Begin studies during the Fall semester. Maintain a GPA of 3.4 or higher. Participate in recruitment activities or mentor Public Service Fellows. Complete required surveys. Submit employment outcome reports before graduation. Why You Should Apply This scholarship program offers far more than tuition support. Participants gain: Access to one of the world s leading international affairs schools. Study opportunities in Washington, DC. Connections with global leaders and policymakers. Leadership development experiences. Career acceleration opportunities. Public service-focused mentorship. Exposure to real-world policy challenges. Graduates are positioned for impactful careers in: Government International Organizations NGOs Public Policy Institutions Human Rights Organizations Development Agencies Think Tanks Diplomatic Services Commitment to Diversity and Inclusion Johns Hopkins SAIS strongly values diversity and welcomes applicants from: Different socioeconomic backgrounds. First-generation students. Historically underrepresented groups. Diverse cultures and nationalities. All genders and identities. The institution believes diverse experiences strengthen public service leadership and policymaking. Information Sessions Prospective applicants are encouraged to attend Public Service Information Sessions hosted by admissions representatives and current students. These sessions provide insights into: Academic programs. Student life. Scholarship opportunities. Career pathways. Financial aid options. Visit HERE to learn more about the Johns Hopkins SAIS Public Service Fellows Scholars Scholarships 2026–2027. Conclusion The Johns Hopkins SAIS Public Service Fellows and Public Service Scholars Scholarships represent a remarkable opportunity for individuals committed to creating meaningful change through public service. With tuition support of up to 100%, access to world-class education in Washington, DC, exclusive leadership programming, career development support, and a global network of policymakers and practitioners, these programs are designed to empower the next generation of leaders tackling today s most complex global challenges. If you are passionate about diplomacy, human rights, development, public policy, climate action, or social justice, this scholarship could be the catalyst that transforms your career and amplifies your impact on the world. Discover more global Scholarship and Fellowship opportunities on OFY. The post Johns Hopkins SAIS Public Service Fellows Scholars Scholarships 2026–2027: Study in Washington DC with Up to 100% Tuition Funding for Future Global Leaders appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: 6 Neuroscience Travel Grants Open in June 2026: Funding Opportunities for Students, Researchers, and Early-Career Neuroscientists Worldwide
summary: For many students, researchers, and early-career neuroscientists, attending an international scientific conference can be a transformative experience. Conferences provide opportunities to present research, receive feedback from experts, build professional networks, discover emerging scientific developments, and establish collaborations that may shape an entire career. However, the cost of conference attendance—including registration fees, travel expenses, accommodation, and meals—can often create barriers for talented researchers, particularly those from low- and middle-income countries. Recognizing this challenge, the International Brain Research Organization (IBRO) is offering multiple travel funding opportunities in June 2026 to support participation in major neuroscience conferences and scientific meetings around the world. These travel grants are designed to help early-career researchers engage with the global neuroscience community, showcase their research, develop professional networks, and gain international scientific exposure. This year, six major neuroscience travel grant opportunities are either open or launching soon, providing support for researchers from Africa, Asia-Pacific, Latin America, and other regions worldwide. Why Neuroscience Travel Grants Matter Scientific conferences play a critical role in career development. Travel grants help researchers: Present their scientific findings to international audiences. Build collaborations with researchers from other institutions and countries. Learn about the latest developments in neuroscience. Receive mentorship and career guidance. Improve visibility within the scientific community. Gain access to new research opportunities. Strengthen academic and professional networks. Explore postgraduate and postdoctoral opportunities. For many early-career researchers, a single conference experience can become a turning point that influences future research directions, funding opportunities, and professional growth. 1. Neuroscience 2026: International Society Member (ISM) Slots Organizer Society for Neuroscience (SfN) Conference Location Washington, D.C., United States Conference Dates 14–18 November 2026 Application Period 15 May – 10 June 2026 Eligible Applicants Applicants from: Africa Asia-Pacific Latin America About the Grant This opportunity supports Society for Neuroscience international members from eligible regions who wish to attend Neuroscience 2026, one of the world s largest neuroscience gatherings. Participants will have the opportunity to: Present their research. Engage with global neuroscience experts. Participate in scientific discussions. Expand professional networks. Explore international collaborations. Key Benefits Access to a major international neuroscience conference. Scientific networking opportunities. Research presentation opportunities. Exposure to global neuroscience developments. 2. KSBNS 2026 Travel Grants Organizer Korean Society for Brain and Neural Science (KSBNS) Conference Location Daejeon, South Korea Conference Dates 6–8 September 2026 Application Period 15 March – 12 June 2026 Eligible Applicants Researchers from the Asia-Pacific region. Maximum Funding Up to USD 300. About the Grant The KSBNS 2026 Travel Grant supports early-career neuroscientists seeking to participate in one of Asia s important neuroscience meetings. The program aims to strengthen regional scientific collaboration while helping researchers gain valuable international exposure. Benefits Financial support for conference participation. Networking with leading neuroscientists. Opportunities to present research findings. Enhanced regional scientific collaboration. 3. Neuroscience 2026: IBRO/SfN Travel Grants Organizer Society for Neuroscience (SfN) Conference Location Washington, D.C., United States Conference Dates 14–18 November 2026 Application Period 15 May – 15 June 2026 Eligible Applicants International applicants worldwide. About the Grant The IBRO/SfN Travel Grant provides support to early-career neuroscience researchers interested in participating in the prestigious Neuroscience 2026 conference. The grant aims to encourage scientific exchange and facilitate engagement with the international neuroscience community. Benefits Participation in a world-class neuroscience conference. International networking opportunities. Exposure to cutting-edge research. Professional development and collaboration opportunities. 4. Neuroscience 2026: IBRO/FUN Travel Awards Meeting Organizer Society for Neuroscience (SfN) Conference Location Washington, D.C., United States Conference Dates 14–18 November 2026 Application Period 1 April – 21 June 2026 Eligible Applicants International applicants worldwide. Available Funding Up to USD 1,000. About the Award This travel award enables researchers to attend one of the largest neuroscience meetings globally while gaining significant scientific visibility and networking opportunities. Required Application Materials Applicants must submit: A Society for Neuroscience abstract. Candidate statement. Faculty sponsor materials. Curriculum Vitae (CV). Benefits Up to USD 1,000 in support. International scientific exposure. Enhanced research visibility. Access to global neuroscience experts. Networking and collaboration opportunities. 5. 2026 Neuroscience Ireland Early Career Researchers Conference Organizer Neuroscience Ireland Conference Location Dublin, Ireland Conference Dates 12–13 August 2026 Application Period 1 April – 30 June 2026 Eligible Applicants International applicants. About the Grant This opportunity supports early-career researchers interested in participating in the Neuroscience Ireland Early Career Researchers Conference. The conference provides an excellent platform for young scientists to exchange ideas, receive feedback on their work, and establish valuable professional relationships. Benefits Scientific exchange opportunities. Networking with peers and experts. Professional development. Exposure to current neuroscience research. 6. Neuroscience Education and Neurotechnology in Nigeria (IBRO/NSN Travel Awards) Organizer Neuroscience Society of Nigeria (NSN) Conference Location Abuja, Nigeria Conference Dates 7–10 September 2026 Application Period 1 June – 30 June 2026 Eligible Applicants Applicants based in Africa. About the Award The IBRO/NSN Travel Awards support participation in the Neuroscience Society of Nigeria Annual Conference. The initiative aims to strengthen neuroscience capacity building across Africa while encouraging collaboration and scientific exchange. Funding Includes Conference registration. Accommodation. Transportation. Meals. Benefits Reduced financial barriers to participation. Access to scientific training and discussions. Networking with African and international neuroscientists. Capacity-building opportunities. IBRO 2027 Travel Fund: Investing in the Future of Neuroscience In addition to the current travel grants, IBRO has launched the IBRO 2027 Travel Fund. This initiative will support travel grants for early-career neuroscientists attending the: 12th IBRO World Congress of Neuroscience Location: Cape Town, South Africa Dates: 13–16 September 2027 The fund seeks to ensure that talented researchers are not excluded from scientific opportunities because of geographical or financial limitations. By supporting the travel fund, donors contribute to creating a more inclusive and globally connected neuroscience community. Why You Should Apply Attending an international neuroscience conference can significantly accelerate academic and professional growth. These travel grants provide opportunities to: Present your work to global audiences. Gain recognition within the neuroscience community. Build long-term collaborations. Learn from leading researchers. Access mentorship opportunities. Increase career prospects. Develop a strong international scientific network. For students and early-career neuroscientists seeking to advance their careers, these travel funding opportunities represent an excellent chance to gain valuable international experience and contribute to the future of neuroscience. Application Deadlines Neuroscience 2026 ISM Slots: 10 June 2026 KSBNS 2026 Travel Grants: 12 June 2026 IBRO/SfN Travel Grants: 15 June 2026 IBRO/FUN Travel Awards: 21 June 2026 Neuroscience Ireland Conference Grant: 30 June 2026 IBRO/NSN Travel Awards: 30 June 2026 Applicants are encouraged to submit their applications as early as possible before the respective deadlines. Official Website For full eligibility requirements and application details, visit: Apply here Learn More For more information about this opportunity, Click here More global Grants opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here The post 6 Neuroscience Travel Grants Open in June 2026: Funding Opportunities for Students, Researchers, and Early-Career Neuroscientists Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Fully Funded PhD Position in Human Geography at the University of Zurich, Switzerland (2026): Join a Groundbreaking SNSF Research Project on Educational Inequality, Racism, and Classism
summary: Are you passionate about social justice, educational equality, youth studies, and qualitative research? The Department of Geography at the University of Zurich (UZH) is inviting applications for a fully funded PhD Position in Human Geography as part of an innovative Swiss National Science Foundation (SNSF) research project examining institutional racism and classism within highly selective secondary schools in Switzerland. This exceptional doctoral opportunity offers aspiring researchers the chance to contribute to cutting-edge research, pursue a doctoral degree within a structured graduate program, engage with young people and educational institutions, and develop an academic career in one of Europe s leading universities. The position is offered at 80% employment and is scheduled to begin on 1 October 2026 or by mutual agreement. About the University of Zurich The University of Zurich (UZH) is the largest university in Switzerland, with approximately 30,000 students and over 10,000 employees. Renowned for its excellence in research, innovation, and teaching, UZH provides an intellectually stimulating environment where doctoral researchers can thrive. Located in Zurich, one of the world s most livable cities, UZH offers outstanding academic resources, international collaboration opportunities, and a vibrant multicultural atmosphere. About the Research Project The successful candidate will join the Swiss National Science Foundation (SNSF)-funded project titled: Institutional Conditions for Racially and/or Socio-Economically Minoritized Students at Highly Selective Secondary Schools in Switzerland. The project is led by Dr. Sara Landolt , Principal Investigator and researcher at the Department of Geography. Research Context Educational systems across many Western societies continue to reproduce social inequalities. In Switzerland, racially and socio-economically minoritized students remain significantly underrepresented in academically selective state-funded secondary schools known as Gymnasia. Existing international research suggests that students from marginalized backgrounds often experience institutional discrimination, including: Racism Classism Social exclusion Structural inequalities Barriers to educational advancement Despite growing awareness of these issues, there remains limited research exploring how institutional racism and classism operate within highly selective educational environments. Main Aim of the Project The project seeks to deepen understanding of: Institutional racism in education Institutional classism The intersection of race and socio-economic inequalities Strategies for preventing discrimination Practices that promote inclusive educational environments The research adopts a post-structuralist processual perspective inspired by the work of Ahmed (2012). Research Objectives The study pursues several important objectives: 1. Investigating the Reproduction of Inequality The project seeks to uncover the underlying mechanisms that reproduce: Racism Classism Intersecting forms of discrimination Resistance to discriminatory practices within Gymnasia in German-speaking Switzerland. 2. Identifying Effective Practices Researchers will explore successful initiatives already implemented within schools to address and reduce: Racism Socio-economic exclusion Educational inequalities 3. Understanding Student Experiences The project aims to understand: How institutional discrimination affects students How students respond to and navigate discriminatory environments Strategies students use to cope with and challenge inequalities 4. Supporting Educational Change Findings will be shared with school leaders to help create educational environments that are free from racism and classism. Responsibilities of the PhD Researcher The selected doctoral researcher will play a central role in the project and will work closely with Dr. Sara Landolt. Key responsibilities include: Research Activities Designing and implementing project activities. Conducting original fieldwork. Carrying out qualitative interviews. Working with school leaders and students. Collecting and managing research data. Fieldwork The researcher will conduct qualitative interviews with: Headteachers of Gymnasia. Racially minoritized students. Socio-economically minoritized students. The fieldwork will primarily take place in German-speaking Switzerland. Data Analysis Responsibilities include: Qualitative data coding and analysis. Participatory data analysis workshops. Interpretation of research findings. Development of evidence-based recommendations. Doctoral Dissertation The successful candidate will complete a paper-based doctoral dissertation based on the project findings. Academic Contributions The researcher will: Participate in project workshops. Attend international conferences. Contribute to scholarly publications. Produce practice-oriented outputs. Engage with stakeholders and educational institutions. Teaching Responsibilities The position also includes contributions to: Human Geography teaching. Geography Teacher Training Unit (GTT) activities. Departmental service responsibilities. Graduate School Participation The doctoral candidate will participate in the: Zurich Graduate School in Geography Earth System Science which provides structured doctoral training and professional development opportunities. Who Should Apply?
